summary: Re-validated ONLY the PDF/XLSX download flow of 'Ventas por Categoría' after E1 fixed localStorage key from 'token' → 'pos_token' on ByCategoryReport.jsx line 9. ALL 7 acceptance criteria PASS on desktop 1920x800: (1) Admin PIN 11338585 + ENTRAR logs in, pos_token present (len=309). (2) Navigated to /reports?report=by-category and data rendered (CERVEZAS RD$10,600 / 37). (3) 'Descargar PDF' triggers GET /api/reports/xlsx/ventas-por-categoria/pdf → status 200, file first bytes start with '%PDF'. (4) 'Descargar Excel' triggers GET /api/reports/xlsx/ventas-por-categoria/xlsx → status 200, file first bytes start with 'PK' (valid XLSX/ZIP magic). (5) Network tab confirmed Authorization: Bearer <jwt> header present on BOTH requests (JWT decoded: role=admin, user=Admin). (6) Success toasts 'PDF descargado' and 'XLSX descargado' displayed. (7) Regression: data-testid uniqueness preserved — by-category-report=1, by-category-table=1, grand-total-row=1, expand-all-btn=1, collapse-all-btn=1, download-pdf-btn=1, download-xlsx-btn=1. No 'Error generando' toast, no console warnings.
action_items: RECOMMENDED (not blocking): Audit sibling report components under /app/frontend/src/pages/reports/ (DailySalesReport, CashCloseReport, TopProductsReport, FormasDePagoReport, etc.) for the same 'token' vs 'pos_token' bug pattern. If any still use localStorage.getItem('token'), their PDF/XLSX downloads will 401 too.
RECOMMENDED: Extract headers() into a shared /lib/auth.js helper to prevent this class of bug.
RECOMMENDED: Replace the silent 'catch {}' in download() with 'catch (err) { console.error(err); ... }' so future 401/5xx errors are visible in console.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix on ByCategoryReport.jsx:9 is correct and minimal — Authorization header now reaches backend as 'Bearer <jwt>' (verified on Playwright request capture).

PDF and XLSX blob responses are handled correctly via responseType:'blob' + URL.createObjectURL + anchor click; downloads saved with correct filename pattern VentasPorCategoria_<from>_al_<to>.(pdf|xlsx).
No duplicate-render regression after the fix; 7 data-testids unique on desktop 1920x800.
Minor: download() still swallows the underlying error silently. Non-blocking.

success_rate.backend: 100% (not re-run; endpoints returned 200 on live integration test)

test_credentials: Admin PIN 11338585 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). JWT stored under localStorage key 'pos_token'.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing jornada data on 2026-04-19 (CERVEZAS RD$10,600 / 37). Default date range had data, so did NOT need to fall back to 2026-04-01 → 2026-04-20.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The 'Ventas por Categoría' PDF/XLSX download regression introduced in iter 150 is now fully resolved. ByCategoryReport.jsx uses localStorage.getItem('pos_token') and the Authorization: Bearer <jwt> header is correctly sent. PDF starts with %PDF, XLSX starts with PK, both status 200. Success toasts work. All 7 data-testids unique. If E1 adds new report components, remind them to use 'pos_token' (not 'token') and ideally extract to a shared auth helper.
rca of the issue: PREVIOUS RCA (iter 150): ByCategoryReport.jsx line 9 read localStorage.getItem('token') but the app stores JWT under 'pos_token' → headers() returned {} → 401. RESOLUTION (iter 151 verified): E1 changed the key to 'pos_token'. Re-test confirmed the Bearer header now reaches the backend and both /pdf and /xlsx endpoints return 200 with valid binary content.
